name: Crockpot Tips 1
recipeInstructions:
LEAVE IT ALONE
The main advantage of slow cooking is to set and forget it. You do not need to tend the pot. By all means, do not hesitate to leave your home while your crockpot is on. Make sure it is not touching the walls or other appliances.
STIRRING
Stirring is not required for the great majority of crock-pot recipes. Occasionally, I encounter one that requires basting, but that is most infrequently.
KEEP IT COVERED
Leave the lid on. Every time the crockpot is uncovered, half the cooking time is lost. Never remove the lid during the first two hours when baking breads or cakes. Most pots have a glass lid, so you can see what's going on inside. If for some reason you must open the pot, make sure you do all your additions then to minimize overall heat loss.
COOKING TIMES
It is best to follow the recipe's instructions, but a general rule of thumb is one hour on high equals two hours on low. Because of the low cooking temperatures, your food will not burn if you cannot be there at exactly the time specified to turn it off.
TEMPERATURES
There are generally two temperature settings: low (200°F) and high (300°F).
FROZEN FOODS
When using frozen foods in a recipe, thawing prior to adding them to the pot is essential. If you have a metal crockpot, you may add frozen items but several extra hours of cooking time must be added.
SPICES
Because so little evaporation takes place when cooking with this method, spices tend to be intensified. I am careful not to over-season and just before serving make my corrections.
ALUMINUM FOIL
To save time, aluminum foil may be placed under the crock pot lid. The foil reflects the heat back onto the food and helps keep the heat in the pot more efficiently.
